62f3ed6a57b6d50239b5329f.question: An allegro moderato section of this work has a brass motif that consists of five sixteenth notes, two eighth notes, one eighth note rest, and one eighth note. The hymn "Be Still, My Soul" is set to music from this work, which was originally the last in a suite of incidental music. This piece, which opens with a menacing second inversion F-sharp minor chord, shares its name with a hall designed by Alvar Aalto, and was performed under the title Impromptu in order to avoid censorship. For 10 points, identify this 1899 work by Jean Sibelius that is named for a Scandinavian country.
62f3ed6a57b6d50239b5329f.question_sanitized: An allegro moderato section of this work has a brass motif that consists of five sixteenth notes, two eighth notes, one eighth note rest, and one eighth note. For 10 points, identify this 1899 work by Jean Sibelius that is named for a Scandinavian country.
62f3ed6a57b6d50239b5329f.answer: <u>Finlandia</u> [or <u>Finland Awakes!</u>; or <u>Suomi heraa</u>; or <u>Impromptu</u> before mentioned]
62f3ed6a57b6d50239b5329f.answer_sanitized: Finlandia [or Finland Awakes!; or Suomi heraa; or Impromptu before mentioned]
